purpose: Field-consolidation dependency map. Records which family field maps depend on which other families' definitions to populate cleanly in Phase 1 Step 4. This is NOT a runtime dependency map; runtime behavior is governed by the validation rules family (VR-01 through VR-30) and the enforcement layers.

globalReferences.description: Every family references enums and validation-rules.

dependencies.rationale: Template manifest binds supported ProjectStage / ProjectType / ProjectStatus enums and references validation rules for decision-closure status.
Settings reference site identity (ProjectId, ProjectNumber) and produce inputs that integrations consume.

Permissions need ProjectStage for phase-based access manager bindings and site identity for project-local SP group naming. Permissions precede site / modules / workflows for invitation and audit contracts.
Site declares the canonical project metadata that pages, libraries, lists, and modules reference.
Pages reference modules by stable identifier (VR-26).
Libraries are scoped to the site and inherit ProjectStatus-driven read-only behavior.
Lists baseline columns (§16.1) reference site identity. Lookups (VR-25) reference other lists.
Modules require site and permissions to declare visibility-by-stage and access-manager bindings.
Workflows seed lists and reference library evidence; lists and libraries must be defined first.
Integrations consume settings (ProcoreCompanyId, M365 posture) and reference site identity. Settings precede integrations.
Site-health depends on every provisioned object family because each named §19.1 check targets one of those families' surfaces.
Provisioning validation references the 15-stage checklist; modules precede provisioning-validation for stage-12 module configuration validation.

notes: Field-consolidation dependencies only. Step 4 schema population will follow this recommended order so each family's referenced family is already populated when its schema is finalized. site-health is last because it depends on every other family.
